questionText: At the sticker shop, four giant sticker packs are labeled 508,407; 508,470; 508,074; and 580,407. A sign says the packs are in order from least to greatest: 508,074; 508,407; 580,407; 508,470. Choose the best judgment about the sign.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can compare and order large numbers
issues: [difficulty_mismatch] | The item is mathematically correct and clear, but its actual cognitive demand is below the stated hard difficulty. | This is an error-finding/order-checking task in context, but it can be solved in about two direct comparison steps, which fits medium more than hard under the provided rules. | Fix: Relabel the item as medium difficulty. | Fix: Or increase complexity to make it truly hard, such as asking students to evaluate multiple claims, identify all errors in a longer ordering, or justify the correction using place-value reasoning across several close numbers.
